response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a versatile full-stack engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js and D language experience, but the role demands deep JVM expertise, Java/Kotlin proficiency, and hands-on work with Spring and enterprise frameworks — none of which are evident in his resume. His only Java exposure appears tangential (robotic arms, brief C/C++ IDE work at NI), and he lists no Kotlin, no Spring, no Quarkus, and no Hibernate. While his work at National Instruments on an IDE (NI LabWindows/CVI) shows some proximity to developer tooling, it was in C/C++ and over a decade ago. The core technical requirements of this role are simply not met.
strengths: Broad systems engineering background spanning web, embedded, mobile, and low-latency services demonstrates adaptability
Brief exposure to IDE internals at National Instruments (NI LabWindows/CVI) and recent MCP/developer tooling work at re:cinq shows genuine interest in developer tooling
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, code katas) align with quality expectations for a large-scale codebase
concerns: No demonstrable Java or Kotlin expertise — Java is listed as a skill but appears only in passing mentions, with no substantial project or professional experience
Zero experience with Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework, which are explicit requirements
Primary technical identity is TypeScript/Node.js and D language, both orthogonal to the JVM-centric toolchain this role requires

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
Tangential IDE and developer tooling exposure
